description: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has authorized durvalumab in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel for the first-line treatment of adult patients with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer who are candidates for systemic therapy, followed by maintenance treatment with durvalumab as monotherapy in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or durvalumab in combination with olaparib in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair proficient (pMMR).

contributions.description: Adding EMA approvals for use to database

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

indication.indication: IMFINZI in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel is indicated for the first-line treatment of adults with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer who are candidates for systemic therapy, followed by maintenance treatment with IMFINZI as monotherapy in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or IMFINZI in combination with olaparib in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair proficient (pMMR).
indication.description: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has authorized durvalumab in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel for the first-line treatment of adult patients with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer who are candidates for systemic therapy, followed by maintenance treatment with durvalumab as monotherapy in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or durvalumab in combination with olaparib in endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair proficient (pMMR).
indication.raw_biomarkers: mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or mismatch repair proficient (pMMR).
indication.raw_therapeutics: Imfinzi (durvalumab) in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel followed by Imfinzi (durvalumab) as monotherapy for endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) or Imfinzi (durvalumab) in combination with carboplatin and paclitaxel followed by Imfinzi (durvalumab) in combination with olaparib for endometrial cancer that is mismatch repair proficient (pMMR).
